action.reasoning: Observation: test_prep succeeds showing ~7k samples with bbox/point targets; train fails immediately on first forward with persistent 'Image features and image tokens do not match (tokens: 476, features: 476)' despite prior debugs showing matching counts; both train.py and predict.py are truncated in workspace (incomplete training loop, no full optimizer/save/metrics in train; incomplete point coord handling + missing JSON write in predict). Analysis: Root cause is inconsistent tensor shapes from Dataset (mix of squeezed/unsqueezed tensors for input_ids/pixel_values/image_grid_thw) combined with incomplete training loop that does not properly re-batch or move to device, leading to bad shapes in Qwen2VL forward (split errors or placeholder mask failure); header search for masking can be brittle but error is pre-loss so template/processor/image_pad count is the real issue - forcing smaller fixed res via resize + explicit debug prints for token/feature counts + complete manual bs=1 loop with explicit unsqueeze will resolve. Predict truncation prevents any eval success after 11 iters. Decision: Overwrite train.py with FULL non-truncated code (Dataset with PIL+system+debug prints for pad count vs merged features from grid_thw, smaller max_pixels+image resize for stability, complete AdamW loop with 64 samples/32 steps, metrics.jsonl, proper save of adapter/processor/grid), complete predict.py fully (finish point/clip/default/JSON output per contract), run test_prep+train then submit_eval to finally get first grounding_score + accurate time measurement before scaling data/epochs/LR.
